title: Bridge Paper: Gut microbiome is associated with insula structure in neonates.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Body-energy** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Interoception — visceral perception, cardiac interoception, insula, vagal signaling, felt aliveness.
**Phase context:** interoception, interoceptive, heart-brain, vagal, insula, cardiorespiratory, breathing entrainment

Paper: Gut microbiome is associated with insula structure in neonates.
DOI: 10.1016/j.physbeh.2025.115001
Abstract: The infant brain and gut microbiome both develop rapidly in early infancy. Growing evidence indicates that the gut microbiome plays a critical role in shaping neurodevelopment early in life, possibly through effects on brain regions involved in affective, interoceptive, and sensory processing. This study used magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) and whole genome sequencing of fecal samples to examine associations between the gut microbiome and brain structures in infants at 2 weeks of age.
